reforms.description: The reforms of industrial relations arrangements in Australia, initiated in 1987 and intensified through the Workplace Relations Act 1996, aimed to enhance productivity by promoting decentralised bargaining and reducing regulatory barriers for small and medium-sized firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing productivity through improved industrial relations is expected to lead to higher economic output.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been in effect since their introduction in 1996.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in industrial relations aimed at improving productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The reforms introduced a large measure of regulatory flexibility in the industrial relations system especially by facilitating and encouraging the setting of wages and employment conditions at the enterprise level.

reforms.description: The Australian Government has introduced legislation to simplify federal awards, aiming to define and specify allowable award matters more clearly, which is expected to enhance regulatory flexibility in workplace arrangements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying federal awards is expected to improve regulatory flexibility, potentially leading to increased productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The legislation has been enacted and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant in enhancing regulatory flexibility but does not represent a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Workplace Relations Amendment (Award Simplification) Bill 2002, if passed, will more clearly define and specify allowable award matters and incidental matters.

reforms.description: The Government introduced the R&D Offset scheme as the principal support mechanism to increase R&D performed by small businesses in Australia, providing a tax rebate for R&D expenditure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax rebate incentivizes small businesses to invest in R&D, which can lead to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been operational since its introduction in 2001.
reforms.importance_rationale: The R&D Offset scheme is a major reform aimed at boosting R&D investment among small businesses.
reforms.source_quote: The R&D Offset scheme is available to all companies with an annual turnover of less than A$5million.
